description: Shifts a vector or line backward by a specified distance opposite to its direction, preserving orientation and magnitude.
utility: Shift a vector backward (opposite direction) by a given distance

arguments.name: vector
arguments.description: The vector or line to shift backward

arguments.name: distance
arguments.description: Distance to shift backward (opposite to vector direction)

alternates.description: Shift vector backward by distance opposite to its direction
Shift line backward by distance (returns vector)

examples.scenario: Shift a vector backward by 2 units
examples.expression:
P1 = point(G, 3, 2)
P2 = point(G, 6, 4)
V = vector(G, P1, P2)
B = backward(G, V, 2)

examples.scenario: Create forward and backward copies of a vector
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
A = point(G, 3, 0)
V = vector(G, O, A)
F = forward(G, V, 2)
B = backward(G, V, 2)

examples.scenario: Shift a line backward as a vector
examples.expression:
A = point(G, 2, 0)
B = point(G, 5, 0)
L = line(G, A, B)
B1 = backward(G, L, 3)

examples.scenario: Backward shift of a vector by 1 unit in vector movements
examples.expression: G=g2d(at(3,6),25,range(-1,5,1),type(geometry)); vector_1 = vector(G, point(G, 2.3, 1.2), point(G, 3.2, 1.7)); bwd_1 = backward(G, vector_1, 1)
